content.problemStatement:
There is a large tree house in an unknown world. It is ruled by the great emperor KZS. It consists of NNN nodes numbered from 111 to NNN in which the people of that world reside. The nodes are organized in a tree structure rooted at node 111. You need to assign values to the nodes according to the wishes of emperor KZS which are as follows :-


The value of node 111 is XXX.
All immediate children of any node have pairwise distinct values.
For every node with at least one immediate child, the gcd⁡\gcdgcd of the values of all immediate children is equal to the value of the node.
The total sum of the values of all nodes should be minimum.
The greatest common divisor gcd⁡(a,b)\gcd(a,b)gcd(a,b) of two positive integers aaa and bbb is equal to the largest integer ddd such that both integers aaa and bbb are divisible by ddd.

Print the sum of all values, modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.

###Input


The first line contains an integer TTT, the number of test cases. TTT testcases follow.
The first line of each test contains two integers NNN and XXX.
Each of the following N−1N-1N−1 lines contains two integers uuu and vvv, denoting an edge between nodes uuu and vvv.
###Output


For each test case, print the sum of values, modulo 109+710^{9} + 7109+7.
###Constraints


1≤T≤151 \leq T \leq 151≤T≤15
2≤N≤3⋅1052 \leq N \leq 3 \cdot 10^{5}2≤N≤3⋅105
1≤X≤1091 \leq X \leq 10^{9}1≤X≤109
1≤u,v≤N1 \leq u, v \leq N1≤u,v≤N and u≠vu \neq vu=v
The given edges form a tree
The sum of NNN over all test cases doesn't exceed 3⋅1053 \cdot 10^{5}3⋅105.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In test case 111, we will give values 111, 222, 333 to the nodes 222, 333 and 444 respectively.
So, the total sum will be 1+1+2+3=71 + 1 + 2 + 3 = 71+1+2+3=7.
